mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, confirming broad-based economic momentum that sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing slowdowns, prompting short-term profit warnings and immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central bank hawkish pivots, generating negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, directly boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show negative mean performance following payroll increases exceeding 150,000 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% and RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence pattern yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds during post-shock recovery phases signal restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain stability, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery regimes fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of historical cases.
